retry_reason: No failure mode; after this put the object may be freed and must not be used again — retrying would cause a double-free.
contract_summary: Release wrapper that decrements the reference count of both a blkg (via blkg_put) and its associated bfq_group (via bfqg_put). This pairs with a prior bfqg_and_blkg_get call. After calling this function, both the blkg and bfqg pointers must be considered potentially freed and must not be dereferenced. The caller must ensure that no other concurrent use of these objects exists, and that any necessary locks (e.g., queue lock, RCU read lock) are held if the put might trigger a free while still in an atomic context. Correct usage requires exactly one put for each previous get, and no further use of the objects afterward.
